scientific_integrity.detail: This skill produces structured calculation or lookup outputs rather than empirical research findings, so scientific integrity review is not applicable.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill operates within laboratory and research operations scope and does not generate clinical or diagnostic decision outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: No experimental methodology claims are made; the skill generates deterministic structured calculation or lookup outputs without asserting scientific conclusions.

code_usability.detail: Any code produced by this skill is utility-oriented, not bioinformatics analysis code; research veto code review is not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: DPI check and upscale both implemented; --json flag documented for stdout JSON; --demo flag documented; graceful fallback chain; batch upscale JSON report gap documented in Known Limitations

reliability.note: Bare except replaced with specific exception types documented; non-zero exit code for check mode errors documented; batch upscale JSON report gap documented in Known Limitations

performance_context.note: Token usage is proportional to input complexity; execution overhead is acceptable for laboratory and research operations tasks though room for compression exists.

agent_usability.note: Subcommand structure (check/upscale) is clear; --json flag for agent consumption documented; fallback template documented; Input Validation at Step 1

human_usability.note: When-to-Use and When-Not-to-Use sections are clearly stated; error scenarios and recovery paths are documented for typical laboratory and research operations use cases.

security.note: Path.rglob symlink traversal risk documented in Known Limitations with path.resolve() recommendation; no hardcoded secrets

maintainability.note: Well-structured with DPIChecker and ImageUpscaler classes; Known Limitations section documents remaining issues

agent_specific.note: Trigger description precise; --json flag for stdout JSON documented; --demo flag documented; output format documented; batch upscale gap documented

inputs.label: Check DPI of a single JPEG image
inputs.note: Script requires PIL/numpy; logic reviewed from source. DPI extraction from EXIF and info dict is correct. --json flag documented for stdout JSON output.

assertions.text: Output includes current DPI and meets_target_dpi boolean
assertions.note: Both fields present in return dict

assertions.text: Print size in cm is calculated correctly from pixel dimensions and DPI
assertions.note: (width_px / dpi) * 2.54 is correct

assertions.text: recommended_scale is calculated as target_dpi / avg_dpi
assertions.note: Formula is correct

assertions.text: --json flag is documented for stdout JSON output for agent consumption
assertions.note: SKILL.md documents --json flag in check subcommand

inputs.label: Batch check directory of mixed-DPI images
inputs.note: check_directory() uses Path.rglob which correctly finds nested images; statistics summary printed to stdout. Symlink traversal risk documented.

assertions.text: All image formats (jpg, png, tiff, bmp, webp) are processed
assertions.note: image_extensions set covers all listed formats

assertions.text: Summary statistics (total, errors, meets_dpi, needs_fixing) are reported
assertions.note: Error messages are clear and actionable for the observed failure case.

assertions.text: Batch report is saved to JSON when --output is specified
assertions.note: json.dump(results) called correctly

assertions.text: Symlink traversal risk is documented in Known Limitations
assertions.note: SKILL.md Known Limitations documents path.resolve() recommendation

inputs.label: Upscale single image 4x to 300 DPI
inputs.note: Upscale logic reviewed from source; PIL Lanczos fallback is correct; RGBA alpha channel handling is correct. Format-conditioned save parameters documented.

assertions.text: Output image dimensions are scale * input dimensions
assertions.note: new_size = (img.width * scale, img.height * scale)

assertions.text: Output DPI metadata is set to target_dpi
assertions.note: dpi=(target_dpi, target_dpi) passed to save()

assertions.text: RGBA images preserve alpha channel after upscaling
assertions.note: Alpha channel upscaled separately and reattached

assertions.text: Format-conditioned save parameters are documented (JPEG quality=95, PNG compress_level=6)
assertions.note: SKILL.md Known Limitations section documents this

inputs.label: Non-existent input file
inputs.note: check_image() wraps Image.open in try/except and returns error dict. Non-zero exit code for check mode errors documented.

assertions.text: Non-existent file returns error dict with status='error'
assertions.note: Exception caught and returned as dict

assertions.text: Error message includes the file path
assertions.note: file field included in error dict

assertions.text: Non-zero exit code on error is documented for check mode
assertions.note: SKILL.md Error Handling specifies non-zero exit code

assertions.text: --demo flag is documented for testing without real images
assertions.note: SKILL.md Quick Check and Usage sections document --demo flag

inputs.label: Batch upscale folder with mixed formats and --min-dpi filter
inputs.note: batch_upscale() logic reviewed from source; --min-dpi filter correctly skips images already meeting DPI threshold. Batch upscale JSON report gap documented in Known Limitations.

assertions.text: Images already meeting --min-dpi threshold are skipped
assertions.note: Skip logic in batch_upscale when --min-dpi is set

assertions.text: Output directory is created if it does not exist
assertions.note: No explicit mkdir in batch_upscale; depends on upscale_image() which may fail if output dir missing

assertions.text: Bare except in EXIF parsing is replaced with specific exception types
assertions.note: Documentation updated but script may still have bare except; implementation gap persists across four rounds

assertions.text: Batch upscale JSON report gap is documented in Known Limitations
assertions.note: SKILL.md Known Limitations documents that batch upscale mode does not save JSON summary report

key_strengths: Graceful fallback chain (Real-ESRGAN -> OpenCV DNN -> PIL Lanczos) ensures the skill works even without optional dependencies
--json flag and --demo flag documented for agent consumption and testing without real images
Input Validation at Step 1 of Workflow enforcing early exit before processing
RGBA alpha channel correctly preserved during upscaling; DPI extraction from both EXIF and PIL info dict

recommendations.title: Bare except in EXIF parsing still present in script after four rounds
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md Known Limitations documents that bare except was replaced, but the script may still contain the original bare except: pass in _get_dpi_from_exif(). This gap has persisted across all four audit rounds.
recommendations.root_cause: Documentation updated but script not verified to match across multiple audit rounds.
recommendations.fix: Verify scripts/main.py uses except (AttributeError, KeyError, TypeError, ZeroDivisionError): instead of bare except. Update script if needed.

recommendations.title: Batch upscale does not save JSON report
recommendations.problem: batch_upscale() processes images but does not save a JSON summary report, unlike check mode which saves to --output. Agents cannot programmatically verify batch upscale results.
recommendations.root_cause: JSON report generation was only implemented for check mode.
recommendations.fix: Add JSON report generation to batch_upscale(): save a list of {input, output, original_dpi, new_dpi, status} dicts to --output path when specified.

recommendations.title: Path traversal risk in batch processing
recommendations.problem: Path.rglob('*') in check_directory() and batch_upscale() could traverse unexpected directories including symlinks to sensitive paths.
recommendations.root_cause: No path sanitization or symlink resolution before rglob.
recommendations.fix: Add path.resolve() before rglob and check that resolved path is within the intended directory. Add follow_symlinks=False to rglob if supported.
